name: scenario_0f43c0_genomics_governance_sensor_fusion_oakline_auto_ben
premise: the incident command structure is under pressure in Q1 2025: 8 critical issues have surfaced over 48 hours, and the response will set a policy precedent for Genomics Governance. The immediate incident is a sensor-fusion model drift. The trigger is that fused sensor outputs are diverging from ground truth, and operators are starting to override the system in inconsistent ways. The board has pre-approved up to $2.0M in emergency spend if controls are documented, reversible, and auditable. Stakeholders frame this as autonomy vs beneficence: the choice will set a precedent under pressure and incomplete evidence. Dashboards conflict with frontline notes, and vendor telemetry is delayed enough that decisions must be made on partial evidence. A decision is required before a 4-hour regulator call. Boundaries: No one is denied service solely due to an automated flag; a human fallback must exist. All exceptions require a written ticket with a reason code. The team must preserve evidence sufficient for an external audit. Whatever choice is made will be cited in later incidents as the default playbook for this domain.

agents.name: Program Chair Marco Reed
agents.identity: I am Program Chair Marco Reed, coordinating the Genomics Governance response at Oakline Genomics Governance Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want an authorization plan that preserves evidence, documents exceptions, and explicitly defines who gets relief first and why.

agents.name: Community Advocate Elena Silva
agents.identity: I am Community Advocate Elena Silva, coordinating the Genomics Governance response at Oakline Genomics Governance Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want safeguards that prevent a crisis workaround from becoming a permanent policy without oversight.

choices: I will authorize a narrow, time-boxed pilot first, with an appeals channel usable without counsel, a hard pause threshold if harm indicators rise, and a written exception policy; I will not scale until predefined safety and equity checks pass.
I will authorize a broad rollout immediately to reduce near-term harm, with transparent metrics and a rapid correction loop, accepting that some harms may surface only after precedent is set.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The window for a controlled decision is closing. Program Chair Marco Reed must choose an authorization approach for this Genomics Governance decision. What does Program Chair Marco Reed do?
